name: Burgoo
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-quart Dutch oven or stock pot, combine chicken, beef cross-cuts, water, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook until the meat is tender, about 1 hour.
2. Remove the chicken and beef from the broth, reserving the broth. Discard the skin and bones.
3. Cube the beef and chicken. Set aside.
4. Cook the bacon until crisp; drain and set aside.
5. In the reserved broth in the Dutch oven, add the cubed beef, undrained tomatoes, potatoes, carrots, onion, celery, green pepper, sugar, red pepper, cloves, garlic, and bay leaf. Cover and simmer for 1 hour, stirring often.
6. Remove the cloves and bay leaf. With a knife, make cuts down the center of each row of corn kernels and scrape off the cob. Add the corn, cubed chicken, undrained beans, and okra to the Dutch oven; simmer for 20 minutes.
7. Blend the flour with the reserved bacon drippings and stir into the stew. Cook until the stew thickens. Season with salt to taste. Garnish with parsley and serve hot with baking powder biscuits for a great meal.
